Functions for managing smartstrap connections, including initialization, monitoring, and subscription.

# Purpose
This C header file provides function declarations for managing connections with smartstraps, which are external devices that can connect to a system. The file includes functions to initialize the connection ([`smartstrap_connection_init`](<#smartstrap_connection_init>)), monitor and manage the connection state ([`smartstrap_connection_kick_monitor`](<#smartstrap_connection_kick_monitor>)), and handle data reception ([`smartstrap_connection_got_valid_data`](<#smartstrap_connection_got_valid_data>)). It also provides utility functions to check the time since the last valid data was received ([`smartstrap_connection_get_time_since_valid_data`](<#smartstrap_connection_get_time_since_valid_data>)) and to determine if there are any active subscribers ([`smartstrap_connection_has_subscriber`](<#smartstrap_connection_has_subscriber>)). Additionally, it includes functions to manage subscription to the smartstrap, allowing the system to connect or disconnect based on the presence of subscribers ([`sys_smartstrap_subscribe`](<#sys_smartstrap_subscribe>) and [`sys_smartstrap_unsubscribe`](<#sys_smartstrap_unsubscribe>)). The file includes necessary headers for standard data types and time functions, ensuring compatibility and functionality within the system.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `kernel/pebble_tasks.h`
- `stddef.h`
- `stdint.h`
- `time.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### smartstrap\_connection\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:smartstrap_connection_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/accessory/smartstrap_connection.h#L25>)

Initializes the smartstrap connection system.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the smartstrap connection system before any other smartstrap-related operations. It prepares the necessary internal structures and resources required for managing smartstrap connections. Call this function once during the initialization phase of your application to ensure that the smartstrap connection system is ready for use.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`smartstrap_connection_init`](<smartstrap_connection.c.md#smartstrap_connection_init>)  (Implementation)

### smartstrap\_connection\_got\_valid\_data<!-- {{#callable_declaration:smartstrap_connection_got_valid_data}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/accessory/smartstrap_connection.h#L32>)

Updates the last valid data reception time for a smartstrap connection.
- **Description**: Use this function to record the time when valid data is received from a smartstrap. This function must be called within the context of the `PebbleTask_KernelBackground` task. It is important for maintaining accurate timing information related to smartstrap data reception.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`smartstrap_connection_got_valid_data`](<smartstrap_connection.c.md#smartstrap_connection_got_valid_data>)  (Implementation)


---
### smartstrap\_connection\_get\_time\_since\_valid\_data<!-- {{#callable_declaration:smartstrap_connection_get_time_since_valid_data}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/accessory/smartstrap_connection.h#L35>)

Returns the time elapsed since the last valid data was received from the smartstrap.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine how much time has passed since the last valid data was received from the smartstrap. This can help in monitoring the connection status or diagnosing communication issues. The function must be called within the context of the Pebble kernel background task, as it asserts this task context.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: The function returns a `time_t` value representing the number of milliseconds since `smartstrap_connection_got_valid_data` was last called.
- **See Also**: [`smartstrap_connection_get_time_since_valid_data`](<smartstrap_connection.c.md#smartstrap_connection_get_time_since_valid_data>)  (Implementation)


---
### smartstrap\_connection\_has\_subscriber<!-- {{#callable_declaration:smartstrap_connection_has_subscriber}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/accessory/smartstrap_connection.h#L38>)

Checks if there are any current subscribers.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if there are any active subscribers to the smartstrap connection. This function is useful for checking the subscription status before performing operations that depend on having subscribers. It must be called within the context of the Pebble kernel background task.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if there is at least one subscriber; otherwise, returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`smartstrap_connection_has_subscriber`](<smartstrap_connection.c.md#smartstrap_connection_has_subscriber>)  (Implementation)


---
### sys\_smartstrap\_subscribe<!-- {{#callable_declaration:sys_smartstrap_subscribe}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/accessory/smartstrap_connection.h#L42>)

Subscribes to the smartstrap service.
- **Description**: Use this function to register interest in the smartstrap service. When at least one subscriber exists, the system will attempt to establish a connection with the smartstrap. This function does not take any parameters and does not return a value. It is typically used in scenarios where interaction with smartstrap devices is required.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`sys_smartstrap_subscribe`](<../../../../../tests/fakes/fake_smartstrap_connection.c.md#sys_smartstrap_subscribe>)  (Implementation)


---
### sys\_smartstrap\_unsubscribe<!-- {{#callable_declaration:sys_smartstrap_unsubscribe}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/normal/accessory/smartstrap_connection.h#L46>)

Unsubscribes from the smartstrap.
- **Description**: Use this function to stop receiving updates or data from the smartstrap. Call it when you no longer need to interact with the smartstrap or want to disconnect. Ensure that no other components require the smartstrap connection before calling this function, as it will disconnect when there are no subscribers.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`sys_smartstrap_unsubscribe`](<../../../../../tests/fakes/fake_smartstrap_connection.c.md#sys_smartstrap_unsubscribe>)  (Implementation)
